popularStory.headline: Life Sketch of Thomas Hull
popularStory.description: Thomas Hull was born about 1552 in Crewkerne, Somerset, England.  He was the next to youngest child of Richard and Alice Hull.  He had five brothers and one sister.  At various times Thomas lived …

burialPlace.address: St. Bartholomew Churchyard, Crewkerne, Somerset, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
THOMAS HULL was born about 1552 in Crewkerne, Somerset, England. He is often referred to as Thomas “the younger” to distinguish him from his older half-bother, Thomas "the elder."

Thomas is the son of Richard Hull and his second wife Alice. 
[Thomas' father was not known as John Richard or Richard Matthew.]

He married Joan(e) Peson [or Pysing] on 11 Jan 1572/73 in Crewkerne. She was the daughter of Richard Pysinge and Margery (last name at birth unknown). 

They had the following children, christened at Crewkerne unless otherwise specified:

1. Rev. William Hull, christened at Winsham 16 November 1574, buried at Colyton 1 June 1627
2. Mary Hull, christened at Winsham 6 October 1576, buried at Crewkerne 6 Sep 1584
3. Agnes Hull, born about 1578, buried at Crewkerne 21 February 1578/9
4. Richard Hull, christened 1 Dec 1579, buried at Crewkerne 27 March 1597
5. Thomas Hull, christened 31 July 1582, death unknown (before 20 May 1627)
6. Robert Hull, christened 8 November 1584, buried at Crewkerne 5 April 1597
7. John Hull, christened 14 Feb 1586/7, buried at Crewkerne 10 October 1627
8. George Hull, born about 1590, died before 25 August 1659 in Fairfield, Connecticut. 
9. Francis Hull, christened 13 Feb 1591/2, death unknown (before 20 May 1627)
10. Joseph Hull, christened 30 Mar 1594, buried at Crewkerne 15 Apr 1595
11. Rev. Joseph Hull, christened 25 Apr 1596, died in Maine, 19 Nov 1665

Note: George Hull was likely baptized at Crewkerne in 1590. One folio (pages 99-100) of the St Bartholomew parish register is missing, covering the period from April to October 1590.

Four of Thomas and Joan's children married:
William Hull, m1. 5 Dec 1603 Jane Sampson, m2. Anne Perkins (named in William's will)
John Hull, m. Edith (unknown). Six children baptized at Crewkerne.
George Hull, m1. 27 Sep 1614 Thamzen Michell. Seven children. m2. Sarah (unknown)
Joseph Hull, m1. about 1619 unknown, seven children. m2. 1633 Agnes Hunt, at least ten children.

Thomas and his family apparently lived briefly in Winsham (6 miles southwest of Crewkerne), since that is where the first two children (William and Mary) were baptized. The third child (Agnes) was buried in Crewkerne and the remaining children were baptized in Crewkerne. So, it appears that they moved to Crewkerne sometime after the baptism of Mary in 1576. 

Thomas was a prosperous yeoman who was taxed at Crewkerne in the Subsidy of Charles I in 1627. 

In the 1630's, Thomas Hull's sons George Hull and Reverend Joseph Hull emigrated from England to the Massachusetts Bay Colony. 

Thomas Hull's will, dated 17 Dec 1636, was proved 10 Feb 1636/7. He left all his goods and chattels to 'my daughter-in-law Edith Hull's four children to be divided between them as shall be most need, by the discretion of John Hull, schoolmaster and John Bere the elder.' Edith was named executrix in trust for the four children, and John Bell and John Beere were witnesses. 

Thomas Hull died in Crewkerne, Somerset, England. He was buried on 29 Dec 1636 at Saint Bartholomew's Church in Crewkerne. 

Sources
Somerset, England, Church of England Baptisms, Marriages, and Burials, 1531-1812," database, Ancestry.com. Original data: Anglican Parish Registers. Somerset Archives & Local Studies, South West Heritage Trust, Taunton, England.
   citing parish registers of Crewkerne and Winsham, Somerset.

Hughes, Phyllis J. Pankonin. "The Hulls of Crewkerne, co. Somerset, England. Part I Richard Hull" Hull Family Association (HFA) Journal, Vol. 12, No. 3, Autumn 2001, Pages 83 – 86

Hughes, Phyllis J. Pankonin. "The Hulls of Crewkerne, co. Somerset, England. Part II Thomas Hull" Hull Family Association (HFA) Journal, Vol. 13, No. 1, Spring 2002, Pages 9 – 14

Hughes, Phyllis J. "Common Errors in the Rev. Joseph Hull Line", The Hull Family Association, hullfamilyassociation.org (accessed 4 Apr 2023)

Wikitree profile of Thomas Hull the younger (https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Hull-478)

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Hull the Younger於大約1552年出生在英國薩默塞特可福可納時，他的父親Sir John Richard Hull43歲，母親Alice Joannah Yeard28歲。他於1572年1月11日與Joan Pysing在英國薩默塞特可福可納結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1636年12月在他的家鄉享年85歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特可福可納。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Hull the Younger于大约1552年出生在英国薩默塞特可福可納时，他的父亲Sir John Richard Hull43岁，母亲Alice Joannah Yeard28岁。他于1572年1月11日与Joan Pysing在英国薩默塞特可福可納结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1636年12月在他的家乡享年85岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特可福可納。

nameMeanings.name: Hull
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hulle, a pet form of Hugh or of its common diminutives Hulin, Hulot (see Hewlett and Huling ).

English: in southwest England and the west and central Midlands sometimes a topographical or habitational name for someone who lived on or by a hill (Middle English atte hulle, from Old English hyll), or from a place with this name. However, this word and the derived names will have usually assumed the standard form Hill in modern times, as in the case of Hill (Gloucestershire), which was usually spelt Hull or Hulle during the Middle Ages. Hull with this origin was also once the name of two other places, now lost, one in Great Budworth (Cheshire), and the other in Inkpen (Berkshire). See also Hell .

English: perhaps a habitational name from Kingston upon Hull in East Yorkshire, which takes its name from the river Hull (perhaps related to Danish hul ‘hole, hollow’, or perhaps a British name based on the root seul- ‘mud’).

variants.name: Huller
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, from Middle English huller (see Hull ). This type of topographic name formation with -er, equivalent to atte Hull, is common in southern England and absent from northern England. See Hiller and Heller .
English: in Yorkshire, a late variant of   Hulley  .
English: variant of Hillier from Middle English huller ‘roofer’, found in Western and southern England as far east as Sussex.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Hull the Younger was born about 1552, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England, his father, Sir John Richard Hull, was 43 and his mother, Alice Joannah Yeard, was 28. He married Joan Pysing on 11 January 1572, in Crewkerne, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 2 daughters. He died in December 1636, in his hometown, at the age of 85, and was buried in Crewkerne, Somerset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Hull the Younger est né environ 1552, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Richard Hull, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Alice Joannah Yeard, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Joan Pysing le 11 janvier 1572, à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en décembre 1636, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Crewkerne, Somerset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Hull the Younger um 1552 in Crewkerne, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Richard Hull 43 und seine Mutter Alice Joannah Yeard 28. Er hat am 11. Januar 1572 in Crewkerne, Somerset, England Joan Pysing geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb im Dezember 1636 im Alter von 85 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Crewkerne, Somerset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hull the Younger è nato il circa 1552 nel Crewkerne, Somerset, England, il suo padre, Sir John Richard Hull, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Alice Joannah Yeard, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Joan Pysing il 11 gennaio 1572, nel Crewkerne, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel dicembre 1636, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 85 anni, e fu sepolto a Crewkerne, Somerset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Hull the Youngerが約1552年にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンで生まれた時，父Sir John Richard Hullは43歳，母Alice Joannah Yeardは28歳であった。1572年1月11日にイギリスソメルセトクレウケルンでJoan Pysingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1636年12月に故郷で85歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトクレウケルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hull the Younger(이)가 대략 1552년 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Richard Hull(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Joannah Yeard(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1572년 1월 11일 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에서 Joan Pysing(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1636년 12월 그의 고향에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 크레w커니에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hull the Younger nasceu aproximadamente 1552, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Richard Hull, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Alice Joannah Yeard, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Pysing em 11 de janeiro de 1572, em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em dezembro de 1636, em sua cidade natal, com 85 anos, e foi sepultado em Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Hull the Younger родился около 1552 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Richard Hull было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Alice Joannah Yeard, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Pysing 11 января 1572 г. в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер декабрь 1636 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности крюкирн, сомирсет, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Hull the Younger nació aproximadamente 1552, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Richard Hull, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Alice Joannah Yeard, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Joan Pysing el 11 de enero de 1572, en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en diciembre de 1636, en su pueblo, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultado en Crewkerne, Somerset, Inglaterra.
